title: celtis biondii f. holophylla
intro: Celtis biondii f. holophylla, also known as a variety of the Chinese hackberry tree, is a plant native to China that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and bark, which contain flavonoids, triterpenoid saponins, and other bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming Celtis biondii f. holophylla is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing cardiovascular risk, and alleviating symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
